text:
| [#210](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/210) | **Retrospective instrumentation** | `tenex-edge explain <handle>` + an `llm_calls` ledger — your "what was fed to the LLM?" question |

**#210** is the one that directly answers your motivating use case: a `tenex-edge explain <handle>` verb over artifact handles (`event:`, `hook:`, `llm:`, `txn:`, `session:@ts`), backed by a persisted `txn_receipts` table and an `llm_calls` ledger that captures the exact transcript slice + system prompt + model + raw response behind every distilled "current activity" — plus `replay` via trace-matching and a redaction policy for the user content in prompts. [...] | [#210](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/210) | **Retrospective instrumentation** | `tenex-edge explain <handle>` + `llm_calls` ledger — your "what was fed to the LLM?" question |

**Two Fable-driven refinements landed in the epic:**
- **Architectural boundary & end-state** — *Trellis owns decisions; the host owns observations and effects.* The world's facts (`ProcessExited`, `DistillCompleted`, `RelayPublishAccepted`, `ClockTick`) enter as a single input journal; Trellis derives sessions/status/who/context/outbox/subscriptions; SQLite becomes a durable log + projection store. Raw transcripts/event bodies stay out — pointers and hashes only.
- **Split-brain risk** — partial adoption is *worse* than today, so each slice must convert **every** writer of a surface in the same change. Shadow mode has no split-brain (nothing is applied); the hazard lives only at promotion — which is exactly why #205 deletes the bespoke path when the close plan turns on, and #206 collapses all triggers at once rather than incrementally.

Slice 8 (#210) is the one that directly answers what started this: point at any published 30315 or injected hook snapshot and recover the exact inputs behind it — including the transcript slice, system prompt, model, and raw LLM response that produced a given "current activity."
